### Book Description

“It would be an endless task to trace the variety of meannesses, cares, and sorrows, into which women are plunged by the prevailing opinion that they were created rather to feel than reason, and that all the power they obtain, must be obtained by their charms and weakness.” —Mary Wollstonecraft  
  
Composed in 1790, Mary Wollstonecraft’s seminal feminist tract _A Vindication of the Rights of Woman_ broke new ground in its demand for women’s education. _A Vindication_ remains one of history’s most important and elegant manifestos against sexual oppression. In her introduction, renowned socialist feminist Sheila Rowbotham casts Wollstonecraft’s life and work in a radical new light.
